name: Backward Lock Step (Back Lock)
preceding_figure_names: Progressive Chasse To Right, Tipsy To L
following_figure_names: Closed Impetus, Tipple Chasse To Right, Running Finish, Tipsy To R
notes:
When one is doing Back Lock, the partner is doing Forward Lock.
The Man (but not the Lady) may use foot rise at the end of 1.The Footwork would be THT, but left heel must not rise abruptly.
The Lady must release the toe of the left foot from the floor as it starts to move back to become step 3.
Body should be backing between wall and DW throughout.
Lock step has zero turn, more rotation. Rotate more to R.

steps.action: crosses in front of RF, Backing DW

name: Change of Direction
preceding_figure_names: Double Reverse Spin, Heel Pivot (Quarter Turn To Left), Reverse Pivot
following_figure_names: Chasse Reverse Turn, Progressive Chasse To Right (step 4 of Change of Direction would not be taken in CBMP when followed by Progressive Chasse To Right), Cross Chasse (at corner)
notes: If you don't pull left hip back, it's easy for the step to become back weighted.

steps.action: fwd | FDW | H | starts to turn L | Sway(S) | CBM
back | BDW | TH | starts to turn L | Sway(S) | CBM
comments: After stepping, pull left hip back, leave L knee fwd, to allow using the standing leg to draw R leg.

name: Closed Telemark
preceding_figure_names: Double Reverse Spin, Heel Pivot (Quarter Turn To Left), Reverse Pivot
following_figure_names: Running Right Turn, Natural Turn And Back Lock, Natural Pivot Turn, Natural Spin Turn, Natural Turn With Hesitation, Natural Turn, Quarter Turn to Right, Forward Lock, Fish Tail, Four Quick Run (at corner, LF back with Lady OP, TH, S, into Four Quick Run), Six Quick Run (at corner, LF back with Lady OP, TH, S, into Six Quick Run)
notes:
Strong big step back, use CBM at the same time.
Do not take shoulders back, do not leave center fwd which will block Man.
R shoulder connect down, R side is anchor for left turning figure. Feel strong upward, leftward through left side (L side slightly raised or heel turns).
Hell turn: drag L heel slowly, don't put down L heel too fast, almost a "S S&" count, put down heel on & and push
When stepping RF, keep contact with Man, step shouldn't be too close to Man. Need to leave room between Lady's RF and Man's LF for Man to step through his next OP step.
Shape head to right (actually by keeping the head where it is, when body turns left, head will be in the shaping right position). Then, head returns to left at next step.
Lowering at end of the step, before driving into next step.

steps.action: Step forward to line of dance, start turn left
Step to side, almost BLOD, with T footwork
Step to side and slightly forward, pointing diagonal wall
Step forward in CBMP, opposite door position
Step back, big step, start turn left
comments: Strong big step back, use CBM at the same time.

steps.action: Do not take shoulders back, do not leave center fwd which will block Man.
comments: Do not take shoulders back, do not leave center fwd which will block Man.

steps.action: R shoulder connect down, R side is anchor for left turning figure.
comments: R shoulder connect down, R side is anchor for left turning figure. Feel strong upward, leftward through left side (L side slightly raised or heel turns)
Hell turn: drag L heel slowly, don't put down L heel too fast, almost a "S S&" count, put down heel on & and push

steps.action: Step to side and slightly back, BDW
comments: When stepping RF, keep contact with Man, step shouldn't be too close to Man. Need to leave room between Lady's RF and Man's LF for Man to step through his next OP step.

name: Cross Chasse
preceding_figure_names: Change of Direction, Double Reverse Spin, Heel Pivot (Quarter Turn To Left), Natural Turn, Reverse Pivot
following_figure_names: Natural Turn And Back Lock, Natural Pivot Turn, Natural Spin Turn, Natural Turn With Hesitation, Natural Turn, Forward Lock, Fish Tail
notes:
Running Cross Chasse
Instead of closing feet above, keep moving ('running') forward:
Leader
S: RF fwd, CBMP, OP | FDW | HT
S: LF fwd | FDW | T | -- | Sway(S) | CBM | Rise e/o 1
Q: RF diagonally fwd | FDW | T | -- | Sway(L) | up
Q: LF fwd, L shoulder leading | FDW | TH | -- | Sway(L) | up, lower e/o 3
S: RF fwd in CBMP, OP | FDW | H | -- | Sway(S) | CBM | --
Follower
S: LF back, CBMP | BDW | TH 
Pay attention not to do a lot rotation through shoulders.
S: RF back | BDW | T | -- | Sway(S) | CBM | Rise e/o 1, NFR 
R shoulder connects down. 
Q: LF diagonally back | BDW | T | -- | Sway(R) | up
Do not rotate L shoulder.
Stretch L side.
Q: RF back, R shoulder leading | BDW | TH | -- | Sway(R) | up
lower e/o 3
S: LF back in CBMP | BDW | T | -- | Sway(S) | CBM | --

steps.action: LF closes to RF | FDW | TH
RF fwd in CBMP, OP | FDW | H
RF closes to LF | BDW | TH
LF back in CBMP | BDW | T

name: Cross Chasse
preceding_figure_names: Change of Direction, Double Reverse Spin, Heel Pivot (Quarter Turn To Left), Natural Turn, Reverse Pivot
following_figure_names: Natural Turn And Back Lock, Natural Pivot Turn, Natural Spin Turn, Natural Turn With Hesitation, Natural Turn, Forward Lock, Fish Tail
notes:
Body is wound up perfect for leading natural turning figures (with first step CBMP , OP).
The figure can be danced continuously or as a "Running Cross Chasse" variation.

steps.action: RF moves forward in open position

comments: Continue into Forward Lock in Outside Partner Position
Maintain the shape, push on RF/thigh.
Styling option: turn head to R

name: Cross Swivel
preceding_figure_names: Double Reverse Spin,Heel Pivot (Quarter Turn To Left),Reverse Pivot
following_figure_names: Tipple Chasse To Right,Running Finish,Fish Tail,Tipsy To R
notes:
Alternatively for styling, RF to side, knees inward
Use hip joint to start the turn, standing knee (R knee) stays forward over the standing foot (RF), do not move the knee off the standing foot.
Use ab muscle to pull to the back of foot, do not leave middle fwd and shoulder weight back.
Heel of RF does not lower until the turn is completed.
Head is still left. At end of &: there is "up" rise feeling before the next S lower to normal.
Body forms a line, slanted straight to R, keep left side lifted. Give R side to Man. Stay connected.
Turn head to R.
At end of &: rise in body before next S to lower to normal. (not "sinking down").

steps.action: RF closes (or nearly closes) to LF slightly back without weight
comments: pressure on T of LF with foot flat and pressure on IE of T of RF

steps.action: LF closes to RF slightly fwd without weight
comments: Use hip joint to start the turn, standing knee (R knee) stays forward over the standing foot (RF), do not move the knee off the standing foot.
Use ab muscle to pull to the back of foot, do not leave middle fwd and shoulder weight back.
Heel of RF does not lower until the turn is completed.
Head is still left. At end of &: there is "up" rise feeling before the next S lower to normal.
At end of &: rise in body before next S to lower to normal. (not "sinking down").

name: Cross Swivel
preceding_figure_names: Double Reverse Spin,Heel Pivot (Quarter Turn To Left),Reverse Pivot
following_figure_names: Tipple Chasse To Right,Running Finish,Fish Tail,Tipsy To R (After LF back with Lady OP, TH, S, into Tipsy to R)
notes: Alternative styling: RF to side, knees inward; LF to side, knee inward; give arms to R; keep left side lifted; give R side to Man; stay connected; turn head to R; rise feeling before next S lower to normal; not sinking down.

steps.action: RF closes to LF slightly back without weight
LF closes to RF slightly forward without weight
comments: Use hip joint to start the turn, standing knee (R knee) stays forward over the standing foot (RF), do not move the knee off the standing foot. Use ab muscle to pull to the back of foot, do not leave middle forward and shoulder weight back. Heel of RF does not lower until the turn is completed. Head is still left. At end of &: there is "up" rise feeling before the next S lower to normal.
Body forms a line, slanted straight to R, keep left side lifted. Give R side to Man. Stay connected. Turn head to R. At end of &: rise in body before next S to lower to normal. (not "sinking down").

name: Double Reverse Spin
preceding_figure_names: Quarter Turn to Left (Heel Pivot), Natural Turn With Hesitation, Reverse Pivot
following_figure_names: Chasse Reverse Turn, Progressive Chasse To Right, Double Reverse Spin, Cross Chasse, Zig-Zag, Back Lock And Running Finish, Quick Open Reverse, Closed Telemark, Cross Swivel, Tipsy To R (LF fwd, H, S into Tipsy to R), Change of Direction
notes:
The Double Reverse Spin may be started LOD (chart above), DC, or DW.
When starting DW, lady: before stepping RF back, turn knee 1/8 to L, step back, so RF is stepping straight down LOD. Body feel quite twisted.
The step 1 of both Double Reverse Spin and Progressive Chasse to Right feel the same, the difference is the next step.
For Double Reverse Spin: Man steps DC, toward Lady's left shoulder (not her sternum).
For Progressive Chasse to Right, Man moves toward LOD, making Lady to step pass her LF to the side.
Double Reverse Spin is a traveling step, first Man goes, then Lady goes. It needs to move down the floor.
The amount of turn may vary between 3/4 and a whole turn.
Complete Turn:
step 1-2: Man 3/8, Lady: 1/2
step 2-3: Man 5/8: Lady: 3/8
step 3-4: lady: 1/8
3/4 Turn:
step 1-2: Man 1/4 (or 3/8), Lady: 3/8
step 2-3: Man: 1/2 (or 3/8), Lady: 1/4
step 3-4: Lady 1/8

comments: Start to rise at end of 1

steps.action: Step to side and slightly back

name: Double Reverse Spin
preceding_figure_names: Heel Pivot (Quarter Turn To Left), Natural Turn With Hesitation, Reverse Pivot
following_figure_names: Chasse Reverse Turn, Progressive Chasse To Right, Double Reverse Spin, Cross Chasse, Zig-Zag, Back Lock And Running Finish, Quick Open Reverse, Closed Telemark, Cross Swivel, Tipsy To R (LF fwd, H, S into Tipsy to R), Change of Direction
notes:
The Double Reverse Spin may be started LOD (chart above), DC, or DW.
When starting DW, lady: before stepping RF back, turn knee 1/8 to L, step back, so RF is stepping straight down LOD. Body feel quite twisted.
The step 1 of both Double Reverse Spin and Progressive Chasse to Right feel the same, the difference is the next step.
For Double Reverse Spin: Man steps DC, toward Lady's left shoulder (not her sternum).
For Progressive Chasse to Right, Man moves toward LOD, making Lady to step pass her LF to the side.
Double Reverse Spin is a traveling step, first Man goes, then Lady goes. It needs to move down the floor.
The amount of turn may vary between 3/4 and a whole turn.
Complete Turn:
step 1-2: Man 3/8, Lady: 1/2; step 2-3: Man 5/8: Lady: 1/4; step 3-4: lady: 1/8.
3/4 Turn:
step 1-2: Man 1/4 (or 3/8), Lady: 3/8; step 2-3: Man: 1/2 (or 3/8), Lady: 1/4; step 3-4: Lady 1/8

comments: Stretch left side and not lean toward inside of the turn.

steps.action: LF closes to RF without weight (Toe Pivot)

comments: Very important to do CBM, with CBM shoulder will clear way for Man to pass. keep shoulder flat, and do not drop it on one side.
Make sure keep body and head very left to Man, keep left side lifted.
Keep the volume of the frame. Do no allow shoulder to rotate too quickly pass the hips, which will pull the Man into Lady's space and making hips rotate away from partner.
Do not take elbow back. Think about giving the arm to Man, keeping it forward throughout the figure. Take R shoulder onto RF, not pass RF.

steps.action: LF closes to RF (Heel turn) | Facing LOD
comments: Draws trailing foot in slowly, both legs should straighten.
Turn head slightly more to left (don't do it too early, though)
Draw center back to spine. Do not leave body to Man.

steps.action: RF to side and slightly back
comments: Bigger step fwd, stay with Man, keep on his R side. Slight swing.
Make this fluid with previous heel turn and use LF to push off, then swivel on RF. Like in Viennese Waltz.
Keeping arms forward, give arms to Man.

steps.action: LF crosses in front of RF
comments: Do not lower too early, lower on "&".

name: Fish–Tail
preceding_figure_names: Closed Telemark, Cross Chasse, Cross Swivel, Forward Lock, Four Quick Run, Progressive Chasse, Running Finish, Running Right Turn, Six Quick Run, Tipple Chasse To Right, V6
following_figure_names: Running Right Turn, Natural Turn And Back Lock, Natural Pivot Turn, Natural Spin Turn, Natural Turn With Hesitation, Natural Turn, Quarter Turn to Right, Forward Lock, Four Quick Run (at corner, LF back with Lady OP, TH, S, into Four Quick Run), V6 (at corner, LF back with Lady OP, TH, S, into 2-12 V6), Tipsy To L (into 2-5 Back Lock down LOD), Six Quick Run (LF back with Lady OP, TH, S, into Six Quick Run)
notes:
The Fishtail may be danced DW with no turn.
Fishtail is often dance after Cross Swivel.
After LF back (Lady shapes to L, RF can cross in front of LF)

LF crosses behind RF, facing LOD, T, 1/8 between 1-2, sway(R), up
RF fwd and slightly to side (small step), facing DW, T, 1/8 between 2-3, sway(S), up
comments: Man lowers while staying on toe at beginning of step, and rises out of it into the forward lock, similar to a tipple chasse.

steps.action: LF diag fwd L side leading, facing DW, T, --, sway(S), up
RF crosses behind LF, facing DW, T, --, sway(S), up
LF diag fwd, facing DW, TH, --, sway(S), up. Lower e/o 6
RF fwd in CBMP, OP, facing DW, T, --, sway(S), CBM, --
LF back in CBMP, backing DC, T, start to turn R, sway(S), CBM, rise at end of 1

name: Four Quick Run
preceding_figure_names: Chasse Reverse Turn, Closed Impetus, Closed Telemark, Fish Tail, Forward Lock, Hover Corte, Natural Spin Turn, Progressive Chasse, Progressive Chasse To Right, Quarter Turn to Right, Quick Open Reverse, Running Finish, Six Quick Run, Tipple Chasse To Right, V6
following_figure_names: Running Right Turn, Natural Turn And Back Lock, Natural Pivot Turn, Natural Spin Turn, Natural Turn With Hesitation, Natural Turn, Quarter Turn to Right, Forward Lock, V6 (at corner, LF back with Lady OP, TH, S, into 2-12 V6), Fish Tail, Tipsy To L (into 2-5 Back Lock down LOD), Six Quick Run (LF back with Lady OP, TH, S, into Six Quick Run)
notes:
Don't be back-weighted, avoid being steppy, keep smooth motion.
Keep left side strongly to the Man all through the steps.
Do not think about rotating upper body to R, which can take spine off standing leg.

name: Hover Corté
preceding_figure_names: Chasse Reverse Turn,Closed Impetus,Natural Spin Turn,Quarter Turn to Right,Quick Open Reverse
following_figure_names: Tipple Chasse To Right,Tipsy To R,Running Finish,Tipsy To L,Four Quick Run (At corner),V6 (At corner, 2-12 of V6),Six Quick Run (At corner)
notes:
Maintain alignment and rise on appropriate beats; avoid excessive sway; keep shoulders, hips, and legs lined; use CBM; lead with right ribs; extension through arm; head can start left then open to right then close.
Preserve shape from lower abdomen up; do not just bend from waist.
Connection lower (hips, upper legs) pressing with partner; allow extension.
Be aware of delayed reaction; man takes head to foot then develops shape.
When giving right ribs/side, tone extends to right arm and hand to man forward.
Begin head can be kept left; later open to right then close and turn left when shaping.

steps.action: LF to side and slightly forward
Transfer weight to RF; side and slightly back
RF to side, LF brushes towards RF

name: Hover Corté
preceding_figure_names: Chasse Reverse Turn, Closed Impetus, Natural Spin Turn, Quarter Turn to Right, Quick Open Reverse
following_figure_names: Tipple Chasse to Right, Tipsy to R, Running Finish, Tipsy to L, Four Quick Run, V6, Six Quick Run
notes: This figure has delayed reaction to Man's body. Man takes head to the foot and then develops the shape out of standing leg. Drive toward the Man, but not shaping too early. This figure has delayed reaction to Man's body. Man takes head to the foot and then develops the shape out of standing leg. To be stable, right shoulder, hip and leg need to be all lined up, weight on RF. R side (ribs) to Man (which will pull hip forward and collect the legs), R hip/middle to Man. Then turn head, stretch/extend. Connection is lower (hips, upper legs is pressing with Man), allowing even more extension. Think of the shape coming from lower part of abdominal first, then taking the curve up to upper body. Don't just bend back from waist and fall back. The side connects to arm. When giving right ribs/side, the tone extends to R arm and give that toned arm/hand to Man forward. At beginning: head can still kept left throughout the figure. When more comfortable, head can be more free, open to the right at end of 1st S, then close and turn to L when developing shaping. After lowering at e/o step 3, hips turn R, makes space to move legs in between. Typically, you breathe out when coming down. S: RF fwd in CBMP; OP | Facing DC against LOD | H -- | Sway(S) | CBM | --

comments: Body turns less. Continue to turn body on step 2.

steps.action: Transfer weight side and slightly back

steps.alignment: Facing center, end facing DC against LOD

name: Natural Pivot Turn
preceding_figure_names: Closed Telemark, Cross Chasse, Fish Tail, Forward Lock
following_figure_names: Natural Turn, Natural Spin Turn, Rumba Cross
notes:
Difference between a Pivot Turn and a Spin Turn is that there is no Rise in Pivot Turn. There is Rise (and brush) in Spin Turn.
At corner: turn 3/8 to face DW on new LOD.
Turn amount is up to 1/2 to face BLOD, followed by any Natural figure.

side step with LF, BDC, turn 1/4 to R, sway
close RF to LF, BLOD, turn 1/8 to R, sway
step back with LF (RF held in CBMP), point BDW, THT, turn up to 1/2 turn, sway
step back, turn R, sway, CBM, start rise, NFR
side step, PLOD, turn 3/8 between 1-2, body turns less, sway L
close LF to RF, FLOD, turn completes, sway L, lower e/o 3
pivot forward (HTH), up to 1/2 to R, sway, CBM

comments: Start to rise at end of 1
Leave head behind, turn body. Continue to keep head to left during the whole Natural Spin Turn.
R side stretched, shape/sway to L.
Keep head to far left, follow L elbow, outside circle. Extend.
Collect center back to the spine.

steps.action: diagonally fwd after brushing to LF
comments: Have the middle of body go forward to Man and keep leg moving, but leaving head behind which creates more extension, then head catches up. Don't pause body movement. Simply just leave head behind.

notes: The step can be underturned to end Man facing new LOD, in which case, 1/4 turn should be completed between 4-5. The heel pull can be underturned to face new LOD. name: Progressive Chasse to Right
preceding_figure_names: Change of Direction, Double Reverse Spin, Heel Pivot (Quarter Turn To Left), Natural Turn With Hesitation, Reverse Pivot
following_figure_names: Closed Impetus, Back Lock, Tipple Chasse To Right, Running Finish, Four Quick Run (when preceding figure ends DC or LOD), V6 (6-12, when preceding figure ends DC or LOD), Tipsy To R, Six Quick Run (when preceding figure ends DC)
notes:
Bronze: The Progressive Chassé to R could be started facing LOD and ended backing DW (3/8 turn) when the amounts of turn would be:
Man: 1/4 between 1-2, 1/8 between 2-3 (body turns less), no further turn
Lady: 3/8 between 1-2 (body turns less), slight body turn to L on 3, no further turn.
Silver and Gold: Started facing DC and ended backing LOD (3/8 turn), or started facing DC and ending backing DC (1/2 turn). If 1/2 turn is mad the amounts of turn will be:
Man: 1/4 between 1-2, 1/8 between 2-3, 1/8 between 4-5
Lady: 3/8 between 1-2 (body turns less). Body completes turn on 3, 1/8 between 3-4 (body turns less)

name: Progressive Chasse to Right

following_figure_names: Closed Impetus, Back Lock, Tipple Chasse To Right, Running Finish, Four Quick Run (when preceding figure ends DC or LOD), V6 (6-12, when preceding figure ends DC or LOD), Tipsy To R, Six Quick Run (when preceding figure ends DC)
notes:
Bronze: The Progressive Chassé to R could be started facing LOD and ended backing DW (3/8 turn) when the amounts of turn would be:
Man: 1/4 between 1-2, 1/8 between 2-3 (body turns less), no further turn
Lady: 3/8 between 1-2 (body turns less), slight body turn to L on 3, no further turn.
Silver and Gold: Started facing DC and ended backing LOD (3/8 turn), or started facing DC and ending backing DC (1/2 turn). If 1/2 turn is mad the amounts of turn will be:
Man: 1/4 between 1-2, 1/8 between 2-3, 1/8 between 4-5
Lady: 3/8 between 1-2 (body turns less). Body completes turn on 3, 1/8 between 3-4 (body turns less)

name: Quarter Turn to Right
starting_position: Closed Position or Outside Partner Position (depending on previous figure)
ending_position: Square, inline, step in between man's legs
preceding_figure_names: Closed Telemark, Fish Tail, Four Quick Run, Progressive Chasse, Running Right Turn, V6
following_figure_names: Progressive Chasse, Reverse Pivot, Heel Pivot (Quarter Turn To Left), Four Quick Run, V6 (2-12, L heel should still lower on preceeding step), Six Quick Run (L heel should still lower on preceeding step), Hover Corte
notes:
If this figure starts facing LOD:
Leader: turn 1/4 to R between 1-2, and 1/8 between 2-3.
Follower: turn 3/8 to R between 1-2, body turns less, finish the turn on 3.
Don't let head and shoulder weight drop back. It's controlled action.

steps.action: RF forward, FDW, HT, start to turn R, sway S, CBM, start to rise e/o 1
comments: Roll your foot, so it's not jerky.

steps.action: LF side, BC, T, 1/8 to R between 1-2, sway R, cont to rise on 2 and 3
RF closes to LF, BDC, T, 1/8 to R between 2-3, sway R, No rise & fall
LF side/slightly back, BDC, TH, --, sway S, Up, lower e/o 4
comments: Don't let head and shoulder weight drop back. It's controlled action.

steps.action: LF back, BDW, TH, start to turn R, sway S, CBM, start to rise e/o 1, NFR
comments: When starting a dance, this is not OP. After initial figure, this will always be OP.

steps.action: RF side, PDC, T, 1/4 to R between 1-2, body turns less, sway L, cont to rise on 2 and 3
comments: Keep head to left, do not allow head and shoulder to start going to the right. In Quarter Turn, the turn happens sooner (compared to heel turns), swing R leg/hip to side. Compared to Natural Turn, this is less effort (less turn).

steps.action: LF close to RF, FDC, T, slight body turn, sway L, --, No rise & fall
comments: In Quarter Turn, there is a check point at end of step 3 (not like Chasse which progressive, trapezoid movement).

steps.action: RF diagonal fwd, FDC, TH, --, sway S, Up, lower e/o 4
comments: Finish square, inline. Step in between Man's legs.

name: Quick Open Reverse
preceding_figure_names: Double Reverse Spin, Forward Lock, Heel Pivot (Quarter Turn To Left), Progressive Chasse, Reverse Pivot, Running Finish, Running Right Turn, Tipple Chasse To Right, V6, Zig-Zag, Back Lock And Running Finish
following_figure_names: Progressive Chasse, Reverse Pivot (after step 3), Four Quick Run, Heel Pivot (Quarter Turn To Left) (after 1-3 of Quick Open Reverse), Hover Corte
notes:
When this is the danced after a Forward Lock, one walk on S is added (Man RF fwd, Lady LF back)
Prepping the walk: get to knees, hip rotate to R, which gives more room to move as well.
When the Progressive Chassé is danced after an underturned Natural Spin Turn or an Impetus Turn as an entry to the Quick Open Reverse, the normal alignment of the Progressive Chassé will be (from Man's view):
1. Backing DC against LOD
2. Pointing DC
3,4,5 Facing DC.
When the Progressive Chassé is taken after a Natural Spin turn danced with normal alignment it will be:
1 Backing DC
2 Pointing to LOD
3,4,5 Facing LOD

comments: A Reverse Pivot may be used on step 4.
R hip is quite forward to Man.

name: Reverse Pivot
preceding_figure_names: Chasse Reverse Turn,Closed Impetus,Natural Spin Turn,Quarter Turn to Right,Quick Open Reverse
following_figure_names: Chasse Reverse Turn,Progressive Chasse To Right,Double Reverse Spin,Cross Chasse,Zig-Zag, Back Lock And Running Finish,Quick Open Reverse,Closed Telemark,Cross Swivel,Tipsy To R (after a step fwd on LF),Change of Direction
notes: If the Reverse Pivot is started backing DC the alignment for the Pivot (Man) would be "to center, toe turned in". Note also that this step is in CBMP owing to the body turning strongly to L as the RF foot slips back.

comments: LF held in CBMP, toe turned in, torso twisted
RF held in CBMP, toe held, posture tall

name: Reverse Pivot
preceding_figure_names: Chasse Reverse Turn, Closed Impetus, Natural Spin Turn, Quarter Turn to Right, Quick Open Reverse
following_figure_names: Chasse Reverse Turn, Progressive Chasse To Right, Double Reverse Spin, Cross Chasse, Zig-Zag, Back Lock And Running Finish, Quick Open Reverse, Closed Telemark, Cross Swivel, Tipsy To R (after a step fwd on LF), Change of Direction
notes: If the Reverse Pivot is started backing DC the alignment for the Pivot (Man) would be "to center, toe turned in". Note also that this step is in CBMP owing to the body turning strongly to L as the RF foot slips back.

comments: LF held in CBMP | DC toe turned in | THT | -- | CBM | --

name: Rumba Cross
preceding_figure_names: Natural Pivot Turn, Natural Turn, Rumba Cross
following_figure_names: Running Right Turn, Rumba Cross
notes: If preceding the Rumba Cross with 1-5 Natural Turn, a sway to L would be used on step 5 which could be held at the beginning of step 1 of the Rumba Cross. This sway assists in maintaining forward movement through the turn. The Rumba Cross may be danced twice. When preceding with a Natural Pivot Turn, S is added before this Q. Lady LF back, continue with the rotation, swing right hip up, back to R, left side is still poised forward (when pivoting), body to Man. head stretch left. Q: RF cross track in the back, activate butt muscle, hip to Man. Even though Lady is going backward, mentally, feel traveling forward (body diagonally left upward), should not be feeling taking body/shoulder back. Q: LF crosses in front of RF | Backing Wall | T | 1/4 between 1-2 | Sway(L) | Up LF almost pulls back straight, not too much room there. Lady's head stays in place, after QQ, as body turns underneath the head, head left. S: RF fwd between partner's feet. End with LF back | Start Facing LOD, End backing LOD | TH | 1/4 between 2-3. | Continue to turn 1/2 on 3 (Pivoting action) | Sway(S) | Up, lower e/o 3 Pivot, head may turn to R (or keep left). needs to be on the foot, ribs to Man. Do not level up shoulders and hips, leaving R side positive. R knee is forward over the toe, completely balanced on RF.

comments: swing right hip up, back to R, left side is still poised forward (when pivoting), body to Man. head stretch left.
activate butt muscle, hip to Man.

steps.action: Forward between partner's feet, ends with LF back
comments: Pivot, head may turn to R (or keep left). needs to be on the foot, ribs to Man.
Do not level up shoulders and hips, leaving R side positive. R knee is forward over the toe, completely balanced on RF.

name: Rumba Cross
preceding_figure_names: Natural Pivot Turn, Natural Turn
following_figure_names: Running Right Turn, Rumba Cross
notes:
Note--If preceding the Rumba Cross with 1-5 Natural Turn, a sway to L would be used on step 5 which could be held at the beginning of step 1 of the Rumba Cross. This sway assists in maintaining forward movement through the turn. The Rumba Cross may be danced twice.
When preceding with a Natural Pivot Turn, S is added before this Q.
Lady's head stays in place, after QQ, as body turns underneath the head, head left.
Do not level up shoulders and hips, leaving R side positive. R knee is forward over the toe, completely balanced on RF.

comments: HT | slight turn to R at end of step

steps.amount_of_turn: turn to R at end of step
comments: T | start to turn R at end of step

Man may turn 1/4 between 1-2 when a facing alignment would be used. Lady would turn 1/8 between 1-2 and 1/8 between 2-3.
Really drive the step, go through Man.
Man should shape left to allow lady to pass and to make the turn easier.
Lady can turn 1/8, will end BDW on new LOD.
Lower and push off standing R leg, bigger next step.

name: Tipple Chasse to Right (after step 4 of a Back Lock) (along side of room)
preceding_figure_names: Back Lock, Cross Swivel, Hover Corte, Natural Turn, Natural Turn And Back Lock, Progressive Chasse To Right, Tipsy To L
following_figure_names: Natural Turn And Back Lock, Natural Pivot Turn, Natural Spin Turn, Natural Turn With Hesitation, Natural Turn, Quick Open Reverse (at corner, when ended DC), Fish Tail (at corner, when ended DC), Tipsy To R (1-4 at corner, then followed by Tipsy to L), Four Quick Run (at corner, LF back with Lady OP, TH, S into Four Quick Run), V6 (2-12, at corner, LF back with Lady OP, TH, S into 2-12 V6), Tipsy To L (then into 2-12 V6 DC or 2-5 Back Lock down LOD), Six Quick Run (at corner, LF back with Lady OP, TH, S into Six Quick Run)
notes: The man begins shaping, bending his spine to the right. After the check step, he returns his spine to the left. The degree to which the direction of movement changes determines how much checking is done on the check step. To continue in a line down the floor, there is hardly any check at all. Silver/Gold: Underturned Tipple Chasse: when starting BLOD after 1-3 of Natural Turn, less turn can be made so this ends with man facing new LOD. Then, step 4 will be to the side, flex knees to check the turn. Continue with 2-5 Forward Lock, moving DC of new LOD. Step 5 as Lady must be taken diagonal back when this alignment is used. Rise will be: slight rise on 2 and 3, lower on 4, commence to rise e/o 4, continue to rise on 5 and 6, up on 7, lower e/o 7. footwork: step 4 will be TH, and IE of T of LF. Sway: to R with head turned to R (Lady to L) may be used. This can be used along the side of the room turning 3/8 on the Chasse to end DC. Follow with Quick Open Reverse or Fishtail.

The man begins shaping, bending his spine to the right. After the check step, he returns his spine to the left. The degree to which the direction of movement changes determines how much checking is done on the check step. To continue in a line down the floor, there is hardly any check at all.
Silver/Gold: Underturned Tipple Chasse: when starting BLOD after 1-3 of Natural Turn, less turn can be made so this ends with man facing new LOD. Then, step 4 will be to the side, flex knees to check the turn. Continue with 2-5 Forward Lock, moving DC of new LOD. Step 5 as Lady must be taken diagonal back when this alignment is used.
Rise will be: slight rise on 2 and 3, lower on 4, commence to rise e/o 4, continue to rise on 5 and 6, up on 7, lower e/o 7.
footwork: step 4 will be TH, and IE of T of LF

name: Open Telemark Natural Turn Outside Swivel Feather Ending
preceding_figure_names: Closed Impetus and Feather Finish, Feather Step, Hover Cross, Hover Feather, Hover Telemark, Natural Hover Telemark, Natural Telemark, Natural Twist Turn, Natural Zig-Zag from PP
following_figure_names: Reverse Turn, Closed Telemark, Open Telemark and Feather Ending, Open Telemark, Natural Turn, Outside Swivel and Feather Ending, Three Step (at corner), Change Of Direction (at corner), Hover Telemark (at corner), Reverse Wave (at corner), Weave from PP (after step 7), Fallaway Reverse and Slip Pivot, Bounce Fallaway with Weave Ending, Natural Zig-Zag from PP (after step 7)
notes:
The Outside Swivel (steps 7-8) could be danced by checking OP on any Feather Movement taken DC and turning 1/4 to R. Follow --Silver--Feather Ending--Natural Turn (when danced as steps 4-6 above)--Natural Weave from PP--Gold--Curved Feather to Back Feather from PP--Natural Zig-Zag from PP. The Outside Swivel (steps 7-8) could also be danced by checking OP on any Feather taken DW without turn and followed as in the previous paragraph.
The Outside Swivel (steps 7-8) could also be danced by checking OP on any Feather taken DW without turn and followed as in the previous paragraph.
When the Outside Swivel is danced at the side of the room to be followed by Feather Ending into Top Spin, 1/8 turn to R is made on step 7 and 8-10 will be taken DC against LOD. Weave from PP may also be danced following this underturned Outside Swivel, Man using the Basic Weave alignment. If dancing this underturned Outside Swivel at a corner steps 8-11 will be taken DC of new LOD. Follow as for Feather.
Follower details include Heel Turn execution, head turning discipline, shaping to left, tracking Man's movement, and maintaining continuous smooth motion without stopping. The Feather finish is achieved through the base with head returning to left.
When the Outside Swivel is danced at the side of the room to be followed by Feather Ending into Top Spin, 1/8 turn to R is made on step 7 and 8-10 will be taken DC against LOD. Weave from PP may also be danced following this underturned Outside Swivel, Man using the Basic Weave alignment. If dancing this underturned Outside Swivel at a corner steps 8-11 will be taken DC of new LOD. Follow as for Feather.
The Outside Swivel (steps 7-8) could also be danced by checking OP on any Feather taken DW without turn and followed as in the previous paragraph.
The Outside Swivel (steps 7-8) could also be danced by checking OP on any Feather taken DW without turn and followed as in the previous paragraph.

steps.action: RF fwd and across in PP and CBMP

comments: Compress right side, leading Lady into swivel action. Rotate through the hip, arms keep rotating across the body to right (arms are still in front of body, not behind.)

steps.action: LF back in CBMP, RF crosses in front without weight
steps.alignment: DW Toe turned in. End facing DC
comments: Keep head poised through diagonal. Diagonal shape is important, do not take head weight back.

LF fwd preparing to step OP L side leading

steps.alignment: Facing DC against LOD (moving to center)
comments: Allow Man to get ahead of you
Left shoulder connects to left hip. Keep shaping to left.
Allow arms to swing across the body.
Then dance straight forward, follow the Man with upper body, legs are tracking straight.

steps.action: RF to side and slightly back

name: Top Spin
preceding_figure_names: Basic Weave, Bounce Fallaway with Weave Ending, Closed Impetus and Feather Finish, Feather Step, Hover Feather, Natural Twist Turn, Natural Weave, Open Telemark and Feather Ending, Reverse Turn
following_figure_names: Reverse Turn, Closed Telemark, Open Telemark and Feather Ending, Open Telemark, Natural Turn, Outside Swivel and Feather Ending, Reverse Wave, Three Step, Change Of Direction, Hover Telemark, Fallaway Reverse and Slip Pivot, Bounce Fallaway with Weave Ending
notes:
On step 1 the footwork of TH may be used. This will often result in a softer and more flowing movement. When this footwork is used the rise will be "Up NFR" on step 2. 
On step 6 of the preceding Reverse Turn with Feather Ending, Man's body will be facing DW and the Lady will be backing DW.
Silver and Gold: Closed Impetus and Feather Finish may be danced turning 1/2 on step 1-3 to back against LOD and 3/8 on Feather Finish to face DC against LOD. Continue with Top Spin to face DW.
Open Telemark, Natural Turn to Outside Swivel may be danced, turning only 1/8 to R for Man, 3/8 for Lady on step 7 to follow with Feather Ending DC against LOD into Top Spin.

steps.action: Backing against LOD, T, 1/8 turn left, sway forward, rise up
comments: On step 1 the footwork of TH may be used.

steps.action: Backing DW Against LOD, T, 1/8 between 1-2, sway forward, CBM, up
To side and slightly forward, pointing DC of new LOD, T, 1/4 between 2-3, body turns less, sway right, rise up
RF forward in CBMP, opposite position, facing DC, sway right, lower at end of 4

name: Weave from PP (after Open Impetus)
preceding_figure_names: Hover Telemark to PP,Open Impetus,Open Telemark, Natural Turn,Outside Swivel and Feather Ending,Three Step,Change Of Direction

comments: offset position of these 3 points must be maintained through PP and weave
Energy still moves diagonal center. Do not think about BLOD.
Connect R shoulder and hip down, Man is inside of turn.
Footwork of TH may be used. The Rise on 5 will then be "Up NFR".
On step 4 the Footwork of TH may be used. The Rise on 5 will then be "Up NFR".

steps.action: forward and across in PP and CBMP
comments: Body doesn't turn till second step.
Head can keep left or right.
Keep knee soft, do not stick tummy to man, pull center back.
R side is the driving side, left side is swinging fwd.
The driving side is always the lower side, not the swinging side.
Swivel the feet. Feet turns more.
